cycleDescription: Annual cycle of an alternative cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin, in which cattle are fed wrapped grass silage, in order to increase their omega-3 fatty acid intake. This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.
title: Effects of type of ration and allocation methods on the environmental impacts of beef-production systems
notes: Authors have used daSilva for soybean cultivation data and dalgaard for soybean processing. Given these data are just proxies, we decided to omit them and just specify that the soybean meal is from Brazil.

siteName: Permanent pasture - Standard Cow-Calf Production System - Charolles (Sous-préfecture), Charolles, Charolles, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France
siteLocation: France - Charolles (Sous-préfecture), Charolles, Charolles, Saône-et-Loire, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France
cycleDescription: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.
title: Effects of type of ration and allocation methods on the environmental impacts of beef-production systems
notes: Authors have used daSilva for soybean cultivation data and dalgaard for soybean processing. Given these data are just proxies, we decided to omit them and just specify that the soybean meal is from Brazil.

cycleDescription: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.
title: Effects of type of ration and allocation methods on the environmental impacts of beef-production systems
notes: Authors have used daSilva for soybean cultivation data and dalgaard for soybean processing. Given these data are just proxies, we decided to omit them and just specify that the soybean meal is from Brazil.

siteName: Animal housing - Italy
cycleDescription: Permanent Meadows Farm - representative of farms located in the Po plain where the cattle were fed principally with forage, compound feed and small quantities of alfalfa and corn feed, without silage feed and protein ingredients. In this system, the forage has spontaneously grown solely in the grassland of the farm and, during warmer seasons, has been left to dry into hay. Therefore, the milk produced is commonly known as “hay milk”, destined to produce Grana Padano cheese as well. Differentiating from the other plain systems, in PMF, meadows were never alternated with other crops, and there was no ploughing or harrowing, only mowing and irrigation. The cattle had the possibility to go in the outdoor paddock during the summer season.
title: The environmental impact of permanent meadows-based farms: A comparison among different dairy farm management systems of an Italian cheese
name: Meat, dairy cattle (liveweight) - Italy - 2019

cycleDescription: Annual cycle of an alternative cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin, in which cattle are fed wrapped grass silage, in order to increase their omega-3 fatty acid intake. This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.
title: Effects of type of ration and allocation methods on the environmental impacts of beef-production systems
notes: Authors have used daSilva for soybean cultivation data and dalgaard for soybean processing. Given these data are just proxies, we decided to omit them and just specify that the soybean meal is from Brazil.

siteName: Animal housing - Italy
cycleDescription: Permanent Meadows Farm - representative of farms located in the Po plain where the cattle were fed principally with forage, compound feed and small quantities of alfalfa and corn feed, without silage feed and protein ingredients. In this system, the forage has spontaneously grown solely in the grassland of the farm and, during warmer seasons, has been left to dry into hay. Therefore, the milk produced is commonly known as “hay milk”, destined to produce Grana Padano cheese as well. Differentiating from the other plain systems, in PMF, meadows were never alternated with other crops, and there was no ploughing or harrowing, only mowing and irrigation. The cattle had the possibility to go in the outdoor paddock during the summer season.
title: The environmental impact of permanent meadows-based farms: A comparison among different dairy farm management systems of an Italian cheese
name: Milk, cow, raw (FPCM) - Italy - 2019

cycleDescription: Annual cycle of an alternative cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin, in which cattle are fed wrapped grass silage, in order to increase their omega-3 fatty acid intake. This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.
title: Effects of type of ration and allocation methods on the environmental impacts of beef-production systems
notes: Authors have used daSilva for soybean cultivation data and dalgaard for soybean processing. Given these data are just proxies, we decided to omit them and just specify that the soybean meal is from Brazil.
name: Beef cattle, calf (male, weaned) - France - 2010

cycleDescription: Annual cycle of the standard cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin. This consists of four components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. 4) Pre-fattening of male calves for 2 months after weaning (before being sent to a separate finishing farm). Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.
title: Effects of type of ration and allocation methods on the environmental impacts of beef-production systems
notes: Authors have used daSilva for soybean cultivation data and dalgaard for soybean processing. Given these data are just proxies, we decided to omit them and just specify that the soybean meal is from Brazil.

cycleDescription: Annual cycle of an alternative cow-calf production system in the Charolais basin, in which cattle are fed wrapped grass silage, in order to increase their omega-3 fatty acid intake. This consists of three components: 1) Reproduction, output of which = weaned male calves, weaned female calves not used for replacement cull cows, cull cows and a breeding bull. 2) Rearing female calves from weaning (9 months) to finishing at 33 months. 3) Finishing of cull cows, i.e. fattened before sending to the slaughterhouse. Both St and O3 are classified as extensive systems with 7.5 months grazing from April to November. The grazed area consists of 88% permanent versus 12% temporary grassland - this has been used to calculate the relative time spent on permanent versus temporary grassland during those 7.5 months of grazing.
title: Effects of type of ration and allocation methods on the environmental impacts of beef-production systems
notes: Authors have used daSilva for soybean cultivation data and dalgaard for soybean processing. Given these data are just proxies, we decided to omit them and just specify that the soybean meal is from Brazil.
